claim: Joe Biden then US former vice president in 2015 pressured the Ukrainian government to fire Viktor Shokin, the top Ukrainian prosecutor, because he was investigating Ukraine’s largest private gas company, Burisma, which had added Biden’s son, Hunter, to its board in 2014.
label: Refuted
justification: There is no evidence that Joe BIden pressured the Ukrainian government to fire Viktor Shokin
speaker: Donald Trump

questions.question: Was Victor Shokin investigating Burisma?

answers.boolean_explanation: In 2015, the U.S. ambassador to Ukraine took the unprecedented step of telling the country it should fire prosecutors who were blocking an investigation of the owner of Burisma, where Hunter Biden was reportedly earning $50,000 a month.

questions.question: Was Hunter Biden part of Burisma Board?

answers.boolean_explanation: That Joe Biden's son Hunter was a member of the board of Burisma was only a decoration of no significance.

questions.question: Why was Victor Shukin fired?

answers.answer:
In the eyes of many Ukrainians, his biggest failure was that nobody from the former regime was prosecuted for the killing of protesters here on Independence Square.
Parliamentarian Yehor Soboliev was the first official to demand the prosecutor's dismissal.

Another reason was failure to investigate grand corruption of Yanukovych. Ukrainian prosecutor general's office didn't want to help to provide evidence.

claim: Moscow, Russia, metro allows free rides to commuters for 30 squats
label: Supported
justification: To promote the winter Olympics the Russian Olympics committee with the Moscow officials allowed passengers to board the train when they do squats
speaker: Aviator Anil Chopra

questions.question: Does Moscow Russia have a scheme where you get free metro ticket if you do exercise to earn it?

answers.answer: In an effort to promote both the then upcoming Winter Olympic Games in Sochi and physical fitness, Moscow city officials and the Russian Olympic Committee are allowing subway riders to pay 30 squats instead of 30 rubles (about 92 cents) for a trip on thetrain.

claim: Barack Obama said, "Anybody can buy any weapon, any time without much, if any, regulation. They buy it over the internet. They can buy machine guns."
label: Conflicting Evidence/Cherrypicking
justification: From the answers and sources the claim is half right as people can buy firearms on the internet but there are regulations in place.
speaker: Barack Obama

questions.question: What are the regulations on the sale of weapons in the US?

answers.answer: There are about 300 federal and state gun laws that regulate the sale and acquisition of firearms in the United States. When it comes to internet gun sales, the firearm is sent to a gun dealer who holds a federal firearms license. An FBI background check is then conducted before the firearm is transferred to the buyer.

questions.question: Can firearms be sold on the internet in the US?

answers.boolean_explanation:
Potential gun buyers can view firearm advertisements and make
purchases from the following categories of websites: major retailers,
online retailers, online auctions and marketplaces, online classified
listings, online forums and social media networks, and Dark Web
websites.

questions.question: Who can buy a machine gun in the US?

answers.answer:
Contrary to popular belief, it is perfectly legal for a law-abiding American citizen to own/possess a machine gun (sometimes called a full-auto firearm or automatic weapon).

The absolute easiest way is for someone to get a Federal Firearms License or “FFL” (even a home-based FFL).

Depending on the type of FFL, the FFL-holder can purchase and sell machine guns, regardless of when they were made (more on this below), and they can even legally make their own machine guns or lawfully convert current firearms into full-autos.

claim: In 2018 we had 10 million tourists who came to our country South Africa, which is a huge increase

questions.question: What was the total number of tourists in South Africa in 2018 between January to November

answers.answer: The total number of tourists was 9, 464, 950

questions.question: What number of tourists visited South Africa in 2017
Is there data for visitors visiting South Africa during 2018?

answers.answer: The data is from January up until November. There is no data for December.

questions.question: When was the claim made?

claim: Kenyan Singer Ringtone Apoko found dead in a waste bin in Karen, Kenya.
label: Refuted
justification: The person who is cliamed to have died on 25th of June 2019 has been reported seen after that date, Q&A state reports are that the person is alive.
speaker: Achilles Gombya

questions.question: When was the claim made?
Has Kenyan singer Ringtone Apoko made any appearances after June 25th, 2019?

answers.boolean_explanation: Ringtone Apoko appeared on Radio Maisha on the 28th of June 2019.

questions.question: Has Ringtone Apoko been in an accident?

answers.boolean_explanation: Ringtone Apoko was in a car accident on the 9th of July 2019, but confirmed that he was not hurt.

questions.question: Has Ringtone Apoko been in a recent accident?

answers.boolean_explanation: Ringtone Apoko was in a car accident on the 9th of July 2019, but confirmed that he was not hurt.

claim: In Kenya, since 2013, county governments have cumulatively received Ksh1.57 trillion worth of equitable share of allocation.
label: Supported
justification: From the Q&A it is found that the amount received is correct, as 1.57 falls between 1.514 to 1.611, so the claim is supported.
speaker: Wycliffe Oparanya

questions.question: How much was actually received by counties from 2012/12 to 2018/19?

answers.answer: The amount actually received by counties is between KSh1.611 trillion and KSh1.514 trillion.

claim: A Facebook meme claims  five House Republicans voted with Democrats to create a “firearms registry.”
label: Refuted
justification: The evidence showcases that the U.S. is forbidden from creating a firearms registry.

questions.question: Is the United states allowed to create a gun registry

answers.answer: The US is forbidden from a creating a gun registry

questions.question: Is the H.R 8 bill tantamount to creating a gun registry

answers.answer: the House bill states that “[n]othing in this Act, or any amendment made by this Act, shall be construed to … authorize the establishment, directly or indirectly, of a national firearms registry.”

claim: U.S. Rep. Maxine Waters made a violence-inciting remark about President Donald Trump and Vice President Mike Pence saying, "I say, we knock off Trump and then, go after Pence. We have the power."
label: Conflicting Evidence/Cherrypicking
justification: U.S. Rep. Maxine Waters was taken out of context. Although she was speaking on the ousting of President Donald Trump, she actually said, "Knock off the first one, and then go after the second one," She did say 'We have the power" when she said, “We have the power. We have the numbers, but we’ve got to understand our power and we’ve got to use our power,”

questions.question: Did U.S. Rep. Maxine Waters  say ''I say, we knock off Trump and then, go after Pence. We have the power.''

answers.boolean_explanation: “Knock off the first one, and then go after the second one,” she said.

questions.question: What was she referring to when she made this statement?

answers.answer: that an ousted President Trump would lead to a President Pence.

questions.question: Was there any context to the statement she made?

answers.boolean_explanation: She went on to say “We have the power. We have the numbers, but we’ve got to understand our power and we’ve got to use our power,”
